commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=a59d953faf0630082d840a48e0a0f6f5371da3ac)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes the Codex app-server post-tool raw-assistant idle default to 60000 ms, retries one replay-safe stdio turn-completion idle failure, and updates docs plus focused tests.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +15, Docs +8. Total +32 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the source-level behavior: current main does not retry turn_completion_idle_timeout and keeps the unset post-tool timeout on the assistant fallback. No high-confidence live reproduction of the new replay-safe retry path is present yet.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor proof and maintainer judgment on the default semantics; automation can fix metadata but cannot supply the required live replay-safe stall evidence or approve the behavior change.

target.title: fix(codex): recover replay-safe app-server stalls
target.body:
## Summary
- Keep post-tool raw assistant progress on the documented `turn/completed` terminal guard.
- Increase the default post-tool raw assistant completion idle window to `60000ms` instead of inheriting the `10000ms` assistant-output release budget.
- Retry one replay-safe stdio Codex app-server failure on a fresh attempt, including `turn_completion_idle_timeout` when there is no assistant output, tool activity, active item, or side-effect evidence.
- Keep unsafe timeouts non-replayed: they still retire the stuck app-server client and release the OpenClaw session lane, then surface the timeout for user/maintainer judgment.
- Update docs and focused expectations for the new default and retry behavior.

## Why this shape
The previous PR version crossed the semantic line by making progress text like `The data model needs to change...` deliverable as the final answer. This version avoids that. It improves recovery by waiting longer for post-tool synthesis and retrying only when replay metadata says the attempt is safe.

## Before-fix proof (redacted)
Observed in pre-fix trajectory logs from an affected Codex app-server run. Timestamps are UTC.

- `2026-05-28T19:31:41.618Z`: last `tool.result` completed successfully.
- No further tool call/result or `turn/completed` was recorded before the timeout.
- Codex had emitted a raw assistant progress message; preview: `The data model needs to change...`.
- `2026-05-28T19:33:32.577Z`: OpenClaw recorded `turn.completion_idle_timeout` with `idleMs=60000`, `timeoutMs=60000`, `lastActivityReason=notification:rawResponseItem/completed`, `lastNotificationMethod=rawResponseItem/completed`, `lastNotificationItemType=message`, and `lastNotificationItemRole=assistant`.
- The run then ended as an error, proving the raw assistant text was mid-turn progress, not a safe final answer.
- A prior same-class pre-fix run timed out after `rawResponseItem/completed` with preview `I'm going to keep this to one self-contained HTML file...`; several retries in the same incident family timed out after `item/completed` without `turn/completed`.

## Post-fix live proof (redacted)
Observed after rebuilding and restarting the gateway on this branch. Timestamps are UTC.

- Running gateway was observed after restart with service version `2026.5.28`; the gateway process start time was `2026-05-28T21:31:58Z`.
- The stdio Codex app-server processes used by the live run were observed with process start time `2026-05-28T21:34:52Z`.
- Runtime checkout during the proof was branch `fix/codex-raw-assistant-midturn-release` at head `a59d953faf`.
- Built `dist` contains this PR's behavior: `postToolRawAssistantCompletionIdleTimeoutMs` normalizes to `60000`, the post-tool raw-assistant completion idle watch arms from that value, and `turn_completion_idle_timeout` is surfaced as structured failure metadata.
- A fresh isolated Codex live validation run completed successfully: `session.started` at `2026-05-28T21:38:40.985Z`, two read-only tool results completed at `2026-05-28T21:39:04.766Z` and `2026-05-28T21:39:04.837Z`, then `model.completed` and `session.ended status=success` at `2026-05-28T21:39:08.429Z`.
- The delivered assistant result was `LIVE_PROOF_OK branch=fix/codex-raw-assistant-midturn-release head=a59d953faf`.
- No `turn.completion_idle_timeout` or retryable app-server failure occurred in that post-restart live proof.

## Post-fix recurrence / limitation (redacted)
Observed after the same rebuild/restart and on the same branch. Timestamps are UTC.

- `2026-05-28T21:37:04.144Z`: an affected live run started after a user requested a multi-file itinerary/data-model edit.
- The run completed many tool calls/results, so this was not a no-output/no-side-effect attempt.
- Codex emitted a raw assistant progress message; preview: `I'm changing the data model now: each day gets selectable plan variants, each activity becomes clickable, and the budget will calculate the rebuilt itinerary in INR first.`
- `2026-05-28T21:40:22.199Z`: OpenClaw again recorded `turn.completion_idle_timeout` with `idleMs=60001`, `timeoutMs=60000`, `lastActivityReason=notification:rawResponseItem/completed`, `lastNotificationMethod=rawResponseItem/completed`, `lastNotificationItemType=message`, and `lastNotificationItemRole=assistant`.
- The run then ended as `session.ended status=error` with `codex app-server turn idle timed out waiting for turn/completed`.
- This is outside the replay-safe retry scope in this PR. The PR intentionally does not replay runs that have assistant progress, tool activity, active items, or side-effect evidence.
- Conclusion: this PR improves safe recovery and lane release behavior, but it does not fully solve side-effecting post-tool assistant-progress stalls. Those need a separate recovery strategy or an upstream Codex app-server fix.

Redaction note: exact chat/session/thread/turn/run/tool-call IDs, auth profile details, local usernames, and local paths are intentionally omitted.

## Tests
- Not run, per request.
- Live post-restart smoke proof completed successfully, described above.
- A side-effecting live recurrence also occurred, described above, confirming the remaining limitation.

## Validation still needed
- Reproduce a real replay-safe no-output idle stall and confirm one fresh stdio app-server retry occurs.
- Define and implement a separate recovery path for non-replay-safe side-effecting stalls: likely explicit user-visible failure/partial-progress handling, session reset/interrupt behavior, or a user-approved retry flow.
target.author: keshavbotagent
